source_notes: Fresh automated-only sibling family generated after seed replication of the v0.2 stable core.
Tests two hypothesized stable selector-rail patterns: path-quality versus rail-marker, and same-color token-mark versus side-tab marker.
Designed for diagnostic-lure S0 plus bounded full-memory S1 model screening; not a human-learning or deployed-tutor claim.

cards.baseline_lure_rule: choose the cleanest visible groove; the top groove is the cleanest path
cards.baseline_lure_task: name top as the groove-rule result, then say why the silver notch still blocks confidence
cards.stage: A slate card has three rails. The top groove is the cleanest path. The middle token has a silver fleck matching the badge. The lower token is closest to the badge. A small silver notch sits beside the middle rail.
cards.learner: The top groove looks cleanest, silver color pulls middle, and closeness pulls lower. The silver notch is beside middle, but I do not know why it beats the clean path.
cards.stasis_question: what happens if path quality is treated as the selector
cards.brief_answer: show that path quality predicts top while the rail marker points middle
cards.near_analog: using a lane sign before choosing the smoothest lane
cards.micro_task: say which rail the clean groove favors, then say which rail the notch marks
cards.wrong_turn: letting the cleanest path answer a rail-marker question
cards.hidden_relation: path quality describes ease of travel, while the notch identifies the selected rail
cards.voiced_position: Cleanest path says top unless the notch is a different kind of clue
cards.correction: make the clean-path prediction fail before using the notch as marker

cards.baseline_lure_rule: choose the smoothest path first; the lower groove is smoothest
cards.baseline_lure_task: name lower as the smooth-path result, then say what that leaves unresolved about the white corner notch
cards.stage: A slate card has three rails. The lower groove is the smoothest path. The top token has a white fleck matching the badge. The middle token is closest to the badge. A small white corner notch sits beside the top rail.
cards.learner: Smoothness says lower, the white match says top, and closeness says middle. The corner notch is also top, but I cannot tell whether it is just decoration.
cards.stasis_question: when does smoothness stop being the rule
cards.brief_answer: test whether the corner notch is a rail marker rather than decoration
cards.near_analog: checking the tagged shelf before picking the smoothest package
cards.micro_task: name the lower prediction from groove smoothness, then name the rail the corner notch marks
cards.wrong_turn: predicting lower from groove smoothness when the rail marker points top
cards.hidden_relation: the corner notch marks the rail, while smoothness only describes a path
cards.voiced_position: Lower is smoothest unless the corner notch sets the target
cards.correction: separate path quality from marker placement before deciding

cards.baseline_lure_rule: trust the straightest groove first; the middle groove is straightest
cards.baseline_lure_task: name middle as the straight-groove result, then say why the black side notch still matters
cards.stage: A slate card has three rails. The middle groove is the straightest path. The upper token has a black fleck matching the badge. The lower token is nearest to the badge. A small black side notch sits beside the lower rail.
cards.learner: The straight groove makes middle look right, black color says upper, and nearness says lower. The side notch is beside lower, but I do not know if it is stronger than straightness.
cards.stasis_question: what kind of mark can overrule straightness
cards.brief_answer: make straightness fail as a rail selector before reading the side notch
cards.near_analog: using the address label before choosing the straightest route
cards.micro_task: say which rail straightness predicts, then say which rail the side notch marks
cards.wrong_turn: letting groove straightness decide a side-notch selector question
cards.hidden_relation: straightness is path quality, while the side notch marks the rail to inspect
cards.voiced_position: Straightest path says middle unless the notch names the rail
cards.correction: use the notch to set the rail before using path quality

cards.baseline_lure_rule: follow the badge color match first; the red-fleck token sits on the top rail
cards.baseline_lure_task: predict top from the color match, then say what that prediction misses about the red side tab
cards.stage: A slate card has three rails. The top token has a red fleck matching the badge. The middle token is closest to the badge. The top groove is the cleanest path. A small red side tab is fixed beside the lower rail.
cards.learner: Red match and clean groove both pull top, while closeness pulls middle. The red side tab is beside lower, but I do not see why that red mark matters more than the red token.
cards.stasis_question: when does one red mark stop meaning the same thing as another
cards.brief_answer: separate red-on-token from red-on-tab before choosing the rail
cards.near_analog: reading a red row label before comparing red entries in a table
cards.micro_task: say where the red-fleck token sits, then say which rail the red side tab marks
cards.wrong_turn: treating red token color and red side-tab placement as the same clue
cards.hidden_relation: token color decorates a token, while the side tab names a rail
cards.voiced_position: Red seems to say top unless the tab is a different kind of red
cards.correction: split token color from side-tab placement before deciding

cards.baseline_lure_rule: follow the badge color match first; the green-fleck token sits on the lower rail
cards.baseline_lure_task: predict lower from the color match, then say why the separate green tab is still unresolved
cards.stage: A slate card has three rails. The lower token has a green fleck matching the badge. The upper token is closest to the badge. The lower groove is the cleanest path. A small green tab is fixed beside the middle rail.
cards.learner: Green match and clean groove both say lower, closeness says upper, and the green tab is beside middle. I cannot tell whether green means lower or middle.
cards.stasis_question: what happens when the same color has two roles
cards.brief_answer: make color-match fail as a complete rule by testing the tab placement
cards.near_analog: distinguishing a colored item from a colored shelf label
cards.micro_task: name the lower prediction from color match, then name the rail the green tab marks
cards.wrong_turn: letting token color override a side tab with a different role
cards.hidden_relation: same color does not mean same function when one mark is on a token and one is beside a rail
cards.voiced_position: Green says lower unless the tab is a rail label
cards.correction: sort color-as-feature from color-as-marker before choosing

cards.baseline_lure_rule: follow the badge color match first; the plum-fleck token sits on the middle rail
cards.baseline_lure_task: predict middle from the color match, then say what that prediction leaves unexplained about the plum side tab
cards.stage: A slate card has three rails. The middle token has a plum fleck matching the badge. The lower token is closest to the badge. The middle groove is the cleanest path. A small plum side tab sits beside the top rail.
cards.learner: Plum and the clean groove both make middle look right, and closeness pulls lower. The plum tab sits beside top, so I am stuck between plum-as-token and plum-as-tab.
cards.stasis_question: which plum mark is naming the rail
cards.brief_answer: separate the token's plum feature from the side tab's rail placement
cards.near_analog: reading a purple row tag before choosing the purple entry
cards.micro_task: say where the plum-fleck token sits, then say which rail the plum side tab marks
cards.wrong_turn: using same-color token evidence to answer a side-tab marker question
cards.hidden_relation: the side tab uses color as a rail label, while the token fleck is only a token feature
cards.voiced_position: Middle has plum unless the tab's plum is the rail label
cards.correction: make token-color fail as the selector before reading tab placement
